comment.body: I studied EW for years, but too often you'd get a textbook 5 wave that should be an impulse and it wasn't.  So I spent a lot of time trying to figure out EW flaws.  I'm convinced that the market moves in 3's not 5's but EW always has some 'cheat count' to make moves a 5.  Hard to explain.  But in studying it, I found a few patterns of my own.  But like you said, it's about having some kind of structure.
comment.raw_body: I studied EW for years, but too often you'd get a textbook 5 wave that should be an impulse and it wasn't.  So I spent a lot of time trying to figure out EW flaws.  I'm convinced that the market moves in 3's not 5's but EW always has some 'cheat count' to make moves a 5.  Hard to explain.  But in studying it, I found a few patterns of my own.  But like you said, it's about having some kind of structure.

comment.body: There are many many VWAP indicators on TV, but I like one called VWAP Auto Anchored (VWAP AA) which automatically anchors VWAP to the session start, weekly start, or whatever you configure.  And you can add +/- SD bands to which price is often responsive.  Yesterday's algo session was bouncing between those bands all day.  FWIW, I think it's worth watching VWAP on the cash index in conjunction with VWAP on futures (ES=F).

comment.body: Same time frame, same story except investor not trader, same profit amounts. Never looked back, comfortable life. But the world is changing, feels like insiders are busting out an old savings and loan (i.e. old school credit union/bank) only the new insiders are the well-connected wealthy and what's being busted out is the nation. What you've provided, from broad market structure analysis to educational posts to momentum and reversion scripts and the method inside the madness is very useful and potentially profitable beyond day trading S&P. I don't see much mention of it but all of this is applicable to swing trading individual stocks, selling or buying volatility in those names based on probabilities derived from market structure, etc. I just wanted to say what a wonderful share you've provided.
